description: This file defines affirmative sentences in the Future Continuous tense in Njo Viri (Balanda). The future continuous tense expresses actions that will be ongoing at a specific time in the future.

general_principle.statement: Future continuous affirmative sentences in Balanda are formed by combining the future marker 'le-', the auxiliary verb 'dho' (be), and the progressive prefix 'ti-' attached to the verb.

affirmative_structure.description: Used to express an action that will be in progress in the future.
constraints: The future marker 'le-' must attach to the auxiliary 'dho'
The progressive prefix 'ti-' must attach directly to the verb base
No other tense or aspect markers may appear

error_constraints: Do not add 'gba' in affirmative sentences

english: I will be eating fish
She will be drinking water
We will be sleeping on the bed
Uku will be going
Ngande will be coming home
They will be walking in the compound
She will be running fast
Uku will be working in the garden
The child will be playing in the compound
I will be watching the dog
I will be speaking Balanda
The girl will be writing words
I will be reading a book
He will be thinking about it
They will be knowing it
I will be asking them
Ngande will be telling them

balanda: Wilii ledho tikeye ti naa ni
english: The girl will be calling her mother
I will be loving you
He will be fearing the snake
She will be hating him
Ngande will be surprising him
He will be seeming calm
The girl will be tying the shoe
He will be cutting the pot
I will be pulling the door
We will be pushing the box
The girl will be washing the shoe
I will be hiding the beads
Ngande will be building a house
I will be creating words
She will be changing the words
I will be turning the door
We will be adding money
I will be describing Balanda
They will be helping the poor
Ngande will be initiating the project
I will be contributing to the project
The girl will be separating trash
We will be upholding the law
I will be wanting water
She will be giving money
I will be finding money
Uku will be trying the cloth
Ngande will be leaving home
I will be arriving in Wau
He will be sharpening the knife
Ngande will be imitating adults
We will be starting the project
He will be falling
